rulings.Case Title: JOHN PELLERITI ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Atlas Heating And Ventilating Company, Ltd.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar, Line 1. Defendant Atlas Heating and Ventilating Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to defendant's argument regarding proper identification of defendant and its employees, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Atlas entity at issue is this defendant. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses, the deposition testimony of Mr. Pelleriti and the Contractors State License Board's license details for other Atlas entities is insufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Pelleriti's deposition testimony and declaration, the deposition testimony of Theresa Meyer and the Atlas websites would create a triable issue whether the Atlas entity at issue is this defendant. As to defendant's duty arguments, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to Mr. Pelleriti. Defendant's arguments based on the doctrine of primary assumption of risk and the sophisticated user doctrine/defense rely on the premise that Mr. Pelleriti was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant only after his training regarding asbestos products and the hazards of asbestos. However defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that premise so it failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to Mr. Pelleriti under either or both theories. Mr. Pelleriti's deposition testimony precludes defendant from sustaining that burden. However even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Pelleriti's deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant only after his training regarding asbestos products and the hazards of asbestos. Further, as to defendant's argument based on the sophisticated user doctrine/defense, even if defendant had demonstrated that it could apply, summary judgment would be unwarranted because it is not a complete defense. It would only preclude plaintiffs' claims based on failure to warn, which defendant did not demonstrate were the sole basis of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Stewart's declaration and Exhibits F, G, H and I (Atlas entity license details) are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits H, I, J and K (deposition excerpts prior to defendant being a party) are sustained and its objections to paragraph 9 of Mr. Pelleriti's declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
